page: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It features 16 shared virtual CPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The storage subsystem consists of a bundled 300 GB SSD. Virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data shows highly polarized performance characteristics. Single-core operations are exceptionally strong, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded performance is more moderate; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark database operations fall within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth is stable, with read-only large block speeds at 124.12 GB/sec and write speeds at 64.65 GB/sec, both ranking in the middle 50%.

The primary tradeoff of this configuration is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce resource contention under sustained multi-core loads, aligning with the average multi-core benchmark results. Additionally, text generation for small language models is slow, registering in the bottom 10% at 13.21 tokens/sec, whereas prompt processing for both small and medium models remains in the middle 50%. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high single-thread efficiency, this server is suited for single-threaded batch processing, web serving, and lightweight database operations.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, equipped with 16 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. It includes a 300 GB SSD and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA_NI. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant contrast: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the top 10% tier, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the middle 50% average. The shared CPU allocation presents a potential bottleneck for sustained parallel workloads. This resource profile is best suited for workloads prioritizing single-thread throughput, such as web serving, lightweight database tasks, and batch processing, rather than sustained multi-threaded computation or real-time LLM text generation.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 KVM server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual server featuring high single-threaded processing speeds, balanced with 32 GB of RAM and 300 GB of SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with 32.0 GB of system memory.
Bundled 300 GB local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address.
Top-tier single-core performance and single-threaded compression speeds.
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results.
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and SHA_NI instruction sets.
Suited for web serving, single-threaded batch jobs, and light database tasks.
